text: Claude Code model configuration | Claude Help Center Claude Code model configuration This guide shows you three ways to change which Claude model you're using with Claude Code: the quick /model command for instant changes, the --model flag for one-time session changes, and environment variables to set your preferred model as the permanent default. Easiest method: Use /model command The simplest way to change models is to use the /model command directly within Claude Code. This works immediately without restarting your terminal. Start Claude Code: claude Start Claude Code: claude Type /model and choose your desired model from the interactive menu. Type /model and choose your desired model from the interactive menu. Your model change takes effect immediately. Your model change takes effect immediately. Note: You can check your current model anytime by running /status in Claude Code. Supported models Opus 4.7, claude-opus-4-7 Opus 4.7, claude-opus-4-7 Sonnet 4.6, claude-sonnet-4-6 Sonnet 4.6, claude-sonnet-4-6 Opus 4.6, claude-opus-4-6 Opus 4.6, claude-opus-4-6 Opus 4.5, claude-opus-4-5-20251101 Opus 4.5, claude-opus-4-5-20251101 Haiku 4.5, claude-haiku-4-5-20251001 Haiku 4.5, claude-haiku-4-5-20251001 Sonnet 4.5, claude-sonnet-4-5-20250929 Sonnet 4.5, claude-sonnet-4-5-20250929 Change model for current session only Use the --model flag when starting Claude Code. Start a fresh Terminal session. Start a fresh Terminal session. Enter the following commands (depending on the model you’d like to use for that session): For Opus 4.7: claude --model claude-opus-4-7 For Sonnet 4.6: claude --model claude-sonnet-4-6 For Opus 4.6: claude --model claude-opus-4-6 For Opus 4.5: claude --model claude-opus-4-5-20251101 For Haiku 4.5: claude --model claude-haiku-4-5-20251001 For Sonnet 4.5: claude --model claude-sonnet-4-5-20250929 Enter the following commands (depending on the model you’d like to use for that session): For Opus 4.7: claude --model claude-opus-4-7 For Opus 4.7: claude --model claude-opus-4-7 For Sonnet 4.6: claude --model claude-sonnet-4-6 For Sonnet 4.6: claude --model claude-sonnet-4-6 For Opus 4.6: claude --model claude-opus-4-6 For Opus 4.6: claude --model claude-opus-4-6 For Opus 4.5: claude --model claude-opus-4-5-20251101 For Opus 4.5: claude --model claude-opus-4-5-20251101 For Haiku 4.5: claude --model claude-haiku-4-5-20251001 For Haiku 4.5: claude --model claude-haiku-4-5-20251001 For Sonnet 4.5: claude --model claude-sonnet-4-5-20250929 For Sonnet 4.5: claude --model claude-sonnet-4-5-20250929 Change default model for all future sessions Step 1) Check your shell type by running: echo $SHELL /bin/zsh → You're using zsh (macOS default) /bin/zsh → You're using zsh (macOS default) /bin/bash → You're using bash (Linux default) /bin/bash → You're using bash (Linux default) Step 2) Add model setting to your shell config: For ZSH users (macOS) Opus 4.7: echo 'export
